---
title: "Bluebook Citation Examples"
subtitle: "Working Citation Cookbook for the GTLS Quarto Legal Template"
author: "Robert C. Cline, CPL"
date: today
date-format: "MMMM D, YYYY"

bibliography:
  - bibliography.bib
---

# Purpose

This document is a working citation cookbook for the GTLS Quarto Legal Template. It is intended to collect tested examples of legal, technical, governmental, and data-source citations that render correctly through Quarto, Pandoc, and the project Bluebook CSL style.

Use this file as a reference when writing research papers in the series *Research Papers on Land, Water, and Mineral Law*.

# Important Quarto Citation Syntax

## Standard citation

Use a normal Pandoc citation when the authority is not named in the sentence:

``` markdown
Nebraska regulates groundwater through statute [@Neb46_701].
```

Example output:

Nebraska regulates groundwater through statute [@Neb46_701].

## Suppress author or authority name

Use `[-@key]` when the authority is already named in the sentence:

``` markdown
Nebraska regulates groundwater through the *Nebraska Ground Water Management and Protection Act* [-@Neb46_701].
```

Example output:

Nebraska regulates groundwater through the *Nebraska Ground Water Management and Protection Act* [-@Neb46_701].

## Multiple citations

Separate multiple citations with semicolons:

``` markdown
Nebraska groundwater regulation involves both statutory authority and local NRD administration [@Neb46_701; @Neb46_739_02].
```

Example output:

Nebraska groundwater regulation involves both statutory authority and local NRD administration [@Neb46_701; @Neb46_739_02].

## Pinpoint citation

For page or section references, add the locator after the key:

``` markdown
A secondary source may be cited to a specific page [@GroundwaterTransfersNebraska, p. 12].
```

Example output:

A secondary source may be cited to a specific page [@GroundwaterTransfersNebraska, p. 12].

# Nebraska Authorities

## Nebraska Statute — Act or Statutory Scheme

Use this form for a statutory act or range of sections.

``` bibtex
@misc{Neb46_701,
  author       = {{Nebraska Legislature}},
  title        = {Nebraska Ground Water Management and Protection Act},
  howpublished = {Neb. Rev. Stat. §§ 46-701 to 46-756},
  year         = {2025},
  note         = {Current through the 2025 legislative session}
}
```

Working citation:

Nebraska regulates groundwater through the *Nebraska Ground Water Management and Protection Act* [-@Neb46_701].

## Nebraska Statute — Specific Section

Use this form for a specific Nebraska Revised Statute section.

``` bibtex
@misc{Neb46_739_02,
  author       = {{Nebraska Legislature}},
  title        = {Recording Transfer of Right to Use Ground Water},
  howpublished = {Neb. Rev. Stat. § 46-739.02},
  year         = {2025},
  note         = {Current through the 2025 legislative session}
}
```

Working citation:

Nebraska law provides for recording an approved transfer of the right to use groundwater [-@Neb46_739_02].

## Nebraska Administrative Rule

Use this form for NRD rules and regulations. Replace the example with the district, chapter, rule, and current date.

``` bibtex
@misc{ExampleNRDRule,
  author       = {{Example Natural Resources District}},
  title        = {Groundwater Management Rules and Regulations},
  howpublished = {Example NRD Rules ch. 5, § 3},
  year         = {2025},
  note         = {Example placeholder; replace with actual NRD rule citation}
}
```

Working citation:

NRD transfer procedures are often governed by district-specific groundwater rules [@ExampleNRDRule].

## Nebraska Supreme Court Case

Use this as a placeholder pattern until a specific Nebraska groundwater case is selected.

``` bibtex
@misc{NebraskaCaseExample,
  author       = {{Nebraska Supreme Court}},
  title        = {Example Nebraska Supreme Court Case},
  howpublished = {000 Neb. 000, 000 N.W.2d 000},
  year         = {2025},
  note         = {Placeholder; replace with actual case name, reporter, and year}
}
```

Working citation:

Nebraska groundwater doctrine should be tested against Nebraska Supreme Court authority [@NebraskaCaseExample].

# Federal Authorities

## United States Code

``` bibtex
@misc{USCExample,
  author       = {{United States Congress}},
  title        = {Example Federal Statute},
  howpublished = {42 U.S.C. § 0000},
  year         = {2025},
  note         = {Placeholder}
}
```

Working citation:

Federal statutory authority may also be relevant in some water-resource contexts [@USCExample].

## Code of Federal Regulations

``` bibtex
@misc{CFRExample,
  author       = {{United States Government}},
  title        = {Example Federal Regulation},
  howpublished = {40 C.F.R. § 000.00},
  year         = {2025},
  note         = {Placeholder}
}
```

Working citation:

Federal regulations may provide technical or administrative standards [@CFRExample].

## United States Supreme Court Case

``` bibtex
@misc{SupremeCourtExample,
  author       = {{United States Supreme Court}},
  title        = {Example United States Supreme Court Case},
  howpublished = {000 U.S. 000},
  year         = {2025},
  note         = {Placeholder}
}
```

Working citation:

Interstate water disputes may require reference to United States Supreme Court original-jurisdiction cases [@SupremeCourtExample].

# Secondary Authorities

## Law Review Article

``` bibtex
@article{LawReviewExample,
  author  = {Doe, Jane},
  title   = {Groundwater Transfers and Local Water Governance},
  journal = {Nebraska Law Review},
  volume  = {100},
  number  = {1},
  pages   = {1--45},
  year    = {2025}
}
```

Working citation:

Secondary legal commentary can help frame statutory and administrative analysis [@LawReviewExample].

## Book

``` bibtex
@book{BookExample,
  author    = {Smith, John},
  title     = {Water Law and Policy in the American West},
  publisher = {Example University Press},
  address   = {Lincoln, Nebraska},
  year      = {2020}
}
```

Working citation:

Books may provide useful background on western water law and groundwater administration [@BookExample].

## Government Report

``` bibtex
@techreport{USGSReportExample,
  author      = {{United States Geological Survey}},
  title       = {Groundwater Conditions in Nebraska},
  institution = {United States Geological Survey},
  year        = {2024},
  number      = {Scientific Investigations Report 2024-0000},
  address     = {Reston, Virginia}
}
```

Working citation:

Government reports are often essential technical authorities for groundwater analysis [@USGSReportExample].

# Electronic and Data Sources

## Government Website

``` bibtex
@misc{GovWebsiteExample,
  author       = {{Nebraska Department of Natural Resources}},
  title        = {Groundwater Data and Information},
  howpublished = {Government website},
  year         = {2025},
  url          = {https://dnr.nebraska.gov/},
  note         = {Accessed July 2, 2026}
}
```

Working citation:

Government websites may provide current administrative data and agency guidance [@GovWebsiteExample].

## NRD Website

``` bibtex
@misc{NRDWebsiteExample,
  author       = {{Example Natural Resources District}},
  title        = {Groundwater Management Program},
  howpublished = {Natural Resources District website},
  year         = {2025},
  url          = {https://www.example-nrd.org/},
  note         = {Placeholder; replace with actual NRD URL and access date}
}
```

Working citation:

NRD websites may describe allocation, certification, transfer, and reporting procedures [@NRDWebsiteExample].

## GIS Dataset

``` bibtex
@misc{GISDatasetExample,
  author       = {{Example County GIS Department}},
  title        = {County Parcel GIS Dataset},
  howpublished = {GIS dataset},
  year         = {2026},
  note         = {Downloaded July 2, 2026; coordinate reference system and processing steps should be documented in the paper}
}
```

Working citation:

GIS datasets should be cited when parcel, well, or spatial analysis supports the paper [@GISDatasetExample].

## R Package

``` bibtex
@manual{RPackageExample,
  title  = {sf: Simple Features for R},
  author = {Pebesma, Edzer},
  year   = {2025},
  note   = {R package},
  url    = {https://r-spatial.github.io/sf/}
}
```

Working citation:

R packages used for reproducible analysis should be cited when they materially support the work [@RPackageExample].

## GitHub Repository

``` bibtex
@misc{GitHubRepoExample,
  author       = {Cline, Robert C.},
  title        = {quarto_legal_template},
  howpublished = {GitHub repository},
  year         = {2026},
  url          = {https://github.com/rccline/quarto_legal_template}
}
```

Working citation:

The publication framework itself can be cited when documenting reproducibility [@GitHubRepoExample].

# Notes for Future Development

Add a new example whenever a new citation type is successfully tested. Each example should include:

1.  The source type.
2.  A working BibTeX entry.
3.  A working Pandoc citation.
4.  A short note explaining when to use it.

# References
